labs/setup.title: Lab Setup
labs/setup.description: Before running any lab, ensure your Kali Linux environment has the required tools installed.

theory/burp-suite.title: Burp Suite — HTTP Interception
theory/burp-suite.description: Burp Suite is a man-in-the-middle proxy that intercepts and manipulates HTTP/S traffic between your browser and a server, making it the go-to tool for web application security testing.

theory/cookies-gdpr.title: Cookies & GDPR
theory/cookies-gdpr.description: Cookie security attributes protect users from XSS and CSRF attacks, while GDPR mandates that consent for non-essential cookies must be freely given and never pre-ticked.

theory/cryptographic-failures.title: Cryptographic Failures
theory/cryptographic-failures.description: Cryptographic Failures (OWASP A02:2021) covers weak algorithms, poor TLS config, insecure password storage, and secrets leaking in code — all of which can expose sensitive data.

theory/csrf.title: CSRF — Cross-Site Request Forgery
theory/csrf.description: CSRF tricks a logged-in user's browser into sending an authenticated request to a target site without their knowledge, because browsers automatically attach cookies to matching requests.

theory/dns-security.title: DNS — Domain Squatting & Certificate Transparency
theory/dns-security.description: Attackers register lookalike domains to impersonate your brand, and Certificate Transparency logs expose subdomains you may not realise are public — both are critical to monitor.

theory/endpoints.title: Endpoint Discovery
theory/endpoints.description: Endpoint discovery is the practice of finding hidden API endpoints and admin panels by guessing URL patterns — and it matters because exposed hidden paths can give attackers direct access to sensitive functionality.

theory/git-exposure.title: .git Exposure — Source Code Leaks
theory/git-exposure.description: An exposed .git/ directory on a web server lets attackers reconstruct the entire source code of an application — including secrets and API keys from the full commit history.

theory/http-headers.title: HTTP Headers & CF-Connecting-IP Spoofing
theory/http-headers.description: If a server blindly trusts IP headers like CF-Connecting-IP or X-Forwarded-For without verifying the request actually came through Cloudflare, attackers can spoof any IP address.

theory/idor.title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
theory/idor.description: IDOR lets an attacker access other users' data simply by changing an ID in the URL — because the server never checks if you're allowed to see it.

theory/injection-xss.title: Injection & XSS
theory/injection-xss.description: Injection attacks insert malicious code into a trusted context; XSS is the browser-side variant where attacker-controlled scripts execute in a victim's browser.

theory/intro.title: Security Theory Overview
theory/intro.description: Welcome to the Security Theory section. These notes are organized from the Niebezpiecznik cybersecurity training (March 2026) and cover OWASP-aligned attack categories.

theory/iot-zero-day.title: IoT, Smart Devices & Zero-day
theory/iot-zero-day.description: IoT devices expand the attack surface with outdated firmware and default credentials, making zero-day vulnerabilities especially dangerous since these devices are rarely patched.

theory/jwt.title: JWT — JSON Web Token
theory/jwt.description: JWT is a three-part base64-encoded token used for authentication, but misconfigurations like weak secrets or the alg:none attack can allow attackers to forge tokens.

theory/path-traversal.title: Path Traversal
theory/path-traversal.description: Path traversal lets an attacker escape the intended directory by injecting ../ sequences into a file path, reaching files the application was never meant to serve.

theory/privilege-escalation.title: Privilege Escalation
theory/privilege-escalation.description: Privilege escalation turns a low-privilege foothold into full system control — often by exploiting misconfigurations, weak sudo rules, or unpatched CVEs.

theory/recon.title: Reconnaissance
theory/recon.description: Reconnaissance is the process of gathering information about a target before attacking — the more you know, the easier the attack.

theory/tls-certificates.title: TLS & Certificates
theory/tls-certificates.description: TLS creates an encrypted channel between client and server using a certificate trust chain — misconfigured TLS exposes users to interception and weakens the security of every feature built on top of it.

tools/burp-suite.title: Burp Suite
tools/burp-suite.description: One-liner: HTTP/HTTPS proxy that intercepts, modifies, and replays web requests between your browser and a server.

tools/dnstwist.title: dnstwist
tools/dnstwist.description: One-liner: Generates domain permutations (typosquatting variants) and checks whether they are registered.

tools/domxss-libs.title: DOMPurify & escape-goat — XSS Defense Libraries
tools/domxss-libs.description: One-liner: Client-side JavaScript libraries for safely inserting user-supplied content into the DOM — preventing XSS at the output stage.

tools/git-dumper.title: git-dumper
tools/git-dumper.description: One-liner: Downloads an exposed .git repository from a web server so you can inspect its full history and contents.

tools/gobuster-dirbuster.title: Gobuster / DirBuster
tools/gobuster-dirbuster.description: One-liner: Brute-force directories and files on web servers using wordlists to discover hidden content.

tools/hashcat.title: Hashcat
tools/hashcat.description: One-liner: GPU-accelerated password hash cracking tool supporting dozens of hash types including JWT, MD5, bcrypt, and NTLM.

tools/http-toolkit.title: HTTP Toolkit
tools/http-toolkit.description: One-liner: Intercepts HTTP traffic with a focus on mobile apps — especially Android via ADB.

tools/hydra.title: Hydra
tools/hydra.description: One-liner: Network brute-force tool for attacking login forms, SSH, FTP, and other authentication services.

tools/intro.title: Tools Overview
tools/intro.description: Quick reference for every tool covered in the Niebezpiecznik training. Each page: what it does, when to use it, install command, and key commands.

tools/jwt-tool.title: jwt_tool
tools/jwt-tool.description: One-liner: Python tool for decoding, analyzing, and attacking JWT tokens — covers all major JWT vulnerabilities.

tools/metasploit.title: Metasploit
tools/metasploit.description: One-liner: Penetration testing framework with hundreds of pre-built exploits for known CVEs in web applications and services.

tools/nikto.title: Nikto
tools/nikto.description: One-liner: Web server vulnerability scanner that finds misconfigurations, default files, outdated software, and dangerous HTTP headers.

tools/sqlmap.title: sqlmap
tools/sqlmap.description: One-liner: Automated SQL injection detection and exploitation tool that can enumerate databases and dump tables.

tools/traitor.title: Traitor
tools/traitor.description: One-liner: Automatic Linux privilege escalation tool that scans for and exploits common escalation vectors after you have shell access.

websites/reference.title: Websites & Online Tools
websites/reference.description: All websites referenced in the Niebezpiecznik cybersecurity training.
